mechanism_narrative: The Federal Reserve's commitment to near-zero interest rates compresses equity discount rates, consistently supporting S&P 500 forward 20-day returns as long as FEDFUNDS remains at the effective lower bound.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of sessions when FEDFUNDS equals 0.05% over a 1-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate from its pandemic peak signals economic recovery, leading to positive S&P 500 forward 60-day returns as labor market healing supports consumption and business investment.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls more than 0.5 percentage points from a 3-month high
failure_condition: If forward 60-day returns are negative in more than half of triggered events during the COVID-19 recovery period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at the effective lower bound and retail sales begin to grow year-over-year, the combination of extreme monetary accommodation and improving consumer demand strongly signals rising equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the forward 20-day return is negative in more than 30% of co-occurrence instances over a 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts is a leading indicator of economic growth, but this signal is only reliable when the Federal Reserve maintains an accommodative stance; otherwise, rising starts may be met with tightening fears that dampen equity returns.
failure_condition: If HOUST increases during periods with FEDFUNDS above 1% produce positive 20-day returns more than 40% of the time, reject regime-conditional claim.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in industrial production reliably indicates economic strength and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market participants price in improving output momentum.
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns after a positive INDPRO print are negative more than 45% of the time over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the Consumer Price Index from depressed levels signals that inflation expectations are normalizing and demand is recovering, which supports equity prices over the next 60 trading days as inflation fears remain muted.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with low inflation base
failure_condition: If forward 60-day returns after trigger are negative in more than 50% of cases over a 3-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in nonfarm payroll employment and retail sales indicate accelerating economic momentum, providing a strong positive signal for S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during the recovery from the pandemic recession.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 0 and RSAFS month-over-month change > 0
failure_condition: If co-occurrence of positive PAYEMS and RSAFS changes leads to negative 20-day returns in more than 25% of instances over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth releases during NBER-dated recessions have outsized predictive power for forward 60-day equity returns because they resolve extreme uncertainty about the depth and duration of the downturn.
failure_condition: If Rank IC of GDPC1 on forward 60-day returns during NBER recessions is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window including the COVID recession.
